eurasian-curlew.size: Large wader; one of the largest in its family, with a notably long downcurved bill
eurasian-curlew.habitat: Breeds on moorlands and grasslands; winters on estuaries, mudflats, and coastal wetlands
eurasian-curlew.diet: Invertebrates including worms, crustaceans, and molluscs probed from mud and soil
eurasian-curlew.migration: Partially migratory; northern breeders move to coasts and southern Europe or Africa in winter

pied-avocet.size: About 40 cm long; wingspan around 70 cm
pied-avocet.habitat: Coastal lagoons, saltmarshes, mudflats, and estuaries
pied-avocet.diet: Aquatic invertebrates swept from water or mudflats with its distinctive upturned bill
pied-avocet.migration: Partially migratory; northern populations winter in southern Europe or Africa, others are resident

eurasian-oystercatcher.size: Large, stocky wader with a long orange-red bill; sexes similar but female's bill is longer
eurasian-oystercatcher.habitat: Coastal shores, estuaries, and increasingly inland rivers and farmland
eurasian-oystercatcher.diet: Bivalves (especially mussels and oysters), worms, and other invertebrates
eurasian-oystercatcher.migration: Partially migratory; many western European birds are resident, others move south in winter

grey-heron.size: Up to 1 m tall; adults weigh 1–2 kg
grey-heron.habitat: Lakes, rivers, ponds, marshes, and sea coasts across temperate Europe, Asia, and Africa
grey-heron.diet: Mostly aquatic prey — fish, frogs, and small mammals caught by standing still or stalking in shallows
grey-heron.migration: Largely resident; northern populations migrate south in autumn

little-egret.size: Small heron; slender and white with a black bill and long black legs
little-egret.habitat: Wetlands, waterways, and shorelines in warm temperate to tropical regions
little-egret.diet: Small fish, amphibians, insects, and other aquatic invertebrates caught in shallow water
little-egret.migration: Partially migratory; northern European birds winter in Africa or southern Asia; southern birds largely resident

eurasian-bittern.size: Large, stocky heron-like bird with cryptic brown streaked plumage
eurasian-bittern.habitat: Reed beds and thick vegetation bordering lakes, rivers, and marshes
eurasian-bittern.diet: Fish, amphibians, small mammals, fledgling birds, crustaceans, and insects
eurasian-bittern.migration: Partially migratory; some northern birds move south in winter, but many are largely resident

eurasian-spoonbill.size: Large wading bird with a distinctive spatula-shaped bill; all-white plumage
eurasian-spoonbill.habitat: Shallow wetlands, estuaries, lagoons, and mudflats across Europe, Africa, and Asia
eurasian-spoonbill.diet: Small fish, crustaceans, and aquatic invertebrates swept up with its spoon-shaped bill
eurasian-spoonbill.migration: Partially migratory; northerly breeding populations migrate south for winter

common-crane.size: Medium-sized crane; one of the tallest birds regularly seen in Europe
common-crane.habitat: Breeds in bogs, wet forests, and marshes; winters on farmland, open fields, and wetlands
common-crane.diet: Omnivorous — seeds, roots, grain, insects, small vertebrates, and invertebrates
common-crane.migration: Strongly migratory; European birds winter in Spain, North Africa, and the Middle East

eurasian-coot.size: Medium-sized waterbird with slaty-black body, white bill, and white frontal shield
eurasian-coot.habitat: Lakes, ponds, reservoirs, slow rivers, and marshes across Europe, Asia, and Australasia
eurasian-coot.diet: Aquatic plants, algae, seeds, and invertebrates; dives and grazes on land
eurasian-coot.migration: Largely resident over most of range; northern birds move south or west in hard winters

common-moorhen.size: Medium-sized rail; smaller than the Eurasian coot, with red-and-yellow bill
common-moorhen.habitat: Well-vegetated marshes, ponds, canals, rivers, and other wetlands
common-moorhen.diet: Omnivorous — plants, seeds, insects, worms, and small invertebrates
common-moorhen.migration: Largely resident across most of range; some northern populations move south in winter

great-crested-grebe.size: Largest grebe in Europe; long neck, distinctive orange-brown and black breeding plumage
great-crested-grebe.habitat: Lakes, reservoirs, and large slow-moving rivers; coastal waters in winter
great-crested-grebe.diet: Primarily fish, caught by diving; also amphibians and aquatic invertebrates
great-crested-grebe.migration: Partially migratory; inland breeders move to coasts or ice-free lakes in winter

whooper-swan.size: Large northern swan; one of the heaviest flying birds
whooper-swan.habitat: Breeds on subarctic lakes, rivers, and marshes; winters on wetlands, flooded fields, and estuaries
whooper-swan.diet: Aquatic plants, grasses, grain, and agricultural crops
whooper-swan.migration: Strongly migratory; breeds in Iceland, Scandinavia, and Siberia; winters in Western Europe and East Asia

greylag-goose.size: 74–91 cm long; average weight about 3.3 kg
greylag-goose.habitat: Moorlands, marshes, lake shores, coastal islands; winters on estuaries, marshes, and flooded fields
greylag-goose.diet: Grasses, roots, grain, and agricultural crops
greylag-goose.migration: Partially migratory; northern populations migrate south in autumn, many southern birds are resident year-round

barnacle-goose.size: Medium-sized black goose with white face; smaller than the greylag goose
barnacle-goose.habitat: Breeds on Arctic cliffs and islands; winters on coastal grasslands and salt marshes
barnacle-goose.diet: Grasses, clover, and other short vegetation grazed on coastal and inland fields
barnacle-goose.migration: Strongly migratory; breeds in Greenland, Svalbard, and Siberia; winters in western Europe

brent-goose.size: Small, compact goose — one of the smallest in the genus Branta
brent-goose.habitat: Breeds on high-Arctic tundra; winters on temperate sea coasts, estuaries, and coastal grasslands
brent-goose.diet: Eel-grass and other coastal vegetation; also grazes on salt marshes and fields
brent-goose.migration: Strongly migratory; breeds in high-Arctic tundra, winters on temperate sea coasts

common-shelduck.size: Large, boldly patterned duck with chestnut breast band and red bill
common-shelduck.habitat: Coasts, estuaries, saline pools, and salt lakes; increasingly on inland freshwater
common-shelduck.diet: Small invertebrates, molluscs, and algae filtered from mud and shallow water
common-shelduck.migration: Partially migratory; many birds moult-migrate to Heligoland Bight; others are largely resident

eurasian-wigeon.size: Medium-sized dabbling duck; male has chestnut head and cream forehead
eurasian-wigeon.habitat: Breeds on lakes and bogs in northern Eurasia; winters on coastal marshes, estuaries, and flooded fields
eurasian-wigeon.diet: Primarily grasses and aquatic plants; grazes on land and dabbles in shallow water
eurasian-wigeon.migration: Migratory; breeds in northern Eurasia, winters across Western Europe, Africa, and southern Asia

common-teal.size: Small dabbling duck; one of the smallest ducks in its range
common-teal.habitat: Sheltered wetlands, marshes, shallow lakes, and rivers with dense vegetation
common-teal.diet: Seeds and aquatic invertebrates obtained by dabbling in shallow water
common-teal.migration: Strongly migratory; breeds in temperate Eurasia, migrates south in winter

tufted-duck.size: Small diving duck with a distinctive drooping crest; population of nearly one million birds
tufted-duck.habitat: Lakes, reservoirs, and slow rivers in northern Eurasia; also coastal bays in winter
tufted-duck.diet: Molluscs, aquatic insects, and plant matter obtained by diving
tufted-duck.migration: Partially migratory; northern birds move south or west in winter

common-pochard.size: Medium-sized diving duck; male has chestnut head and grey body
common-pochard.habitat: Breeds on steppe lakes and Scandinavian waters; winters on large lakes, reservoirs, and estuaries
common-pochard.diet: Aquatic plants, molluscs, and invertebrates obtained by diving
common-pochard.migration: Migratory; breeds in Scandinavia and Siberia, winters farther south and west in Europe

common-eider.size: 50–71 cm long; large sea-duck, one of the heaviest ducks in its range
common-eider.habitat: Arctic and northern temperate coasts; breeds close to the sea, winters on coastal waters
common-eider.diet: Molluscs, crustaceans, and other marine invertebrates obtained by diving
common-eider.migration: Partially migratory; breeds in the Arctic, winters somewhat farther south on temperate coasts

red-breasted-merganser.size: Medium-sized diving duck with a serrated bill and, in males, a red-brown breast in breeding plumage
red-breasted-merganser.habitat: Breeds on lakes and rivers; winters mostly on coastal saltwater bays and estuaries
red-breasted-merganser.diet: Fish caught underwater using serrated bills; dives from the surface to pursue prey
red-breasted-merganser.migration: Migratory; moves from inland breeding lakes and rivers to coastal wintering areas each year

goosander.size: Large sea duck; the largest species of merganser
goosander.habitat: Rivers and lakes in forested areas of Europe, Asia, and North America
goosander.diet: Mainly fish, caught by diving; nests in holes in riverside trees
goosander.migration: Partially migratory; some birds move to lower-altitude or coastal waters in winter

white-tailed-eagle.size: Large eagle; one of the largest in Eurasia with a very broad wingspan
white-tailed-eagle.habitat: Coastal areas, large rivers, lakes, wetlands, and bogs; requires old-growth trees or cliffs for nesting
white-tailed-eagle.diet: Fish and water birds primarily; also mammals and carrion as an opportunistic predator and scavenger
white-tailed-eagle.migration: Largely resident; some northern or eastern birds move south in winter

western-marsh-harrier.size: Large harrier; the heaviest and biggest of the European harriers
western-marsh-harrier.habitat: Reed beds, marshes, and wetlands of temperate and subtropical western Eurasia and Africa
western-marsh-harrier.diet: Small birds, mammals, frogs, and other wetland prey caught in low hunting flights over reedbeds
western-marsh-harrier.migration: Largely migratory; most birds migrate south to Africa for winter; North African race is resident

eurasian-sparrowhawk.size: Small bird of prey; female up to 25% larger than male — one of the greatest size differences in any bird
eurasian-sparrowhawk.habitat: Woodland of any type for breeding; also hunts in gardens and urban areas
eurasian-sparrowhawk.diet: Woodland birds — males take small birds like tits and finches; females take thrushes and starlings up to 500 g
eurasian-sparrowhawk.migration: Partially migratory; northern birds migrate south in winter, southern birds are largely resident

northern-goshawk.size: Medium-large raptor; the largest accipiter in its range
northern-goshawk.habitat: Temperate forests across Eurasia; mostly found in mature woodland
northern-goshawk.diet: Birds and mammals up to the size of hares and pheasants, caught in rapid woodland pursuits
northern-goshawk.migration: Mainly resident; birds from colder northern regions migrate south for the winter

common-buzzard.size: Medium-sized raptor; the most abundant diurnal bird of prey in Europe
common-buzzard.habitat: Most habitats with trees for nesting, including woodland, farmland, and upland areas
common-buzzard.diet: Mostly small mammals, especially voles and rabbits; also birds, amphibians, and carrion
common-buzzard.migration: Largely resident over much of range; birds from colder northern and eastern areas migrate south in winter

red-kite.size: Medium-large raptor with a distinctive forked tail and rufous-brown plumage
red-kite.habitat: Woodland, farmland, and open country across Europe; often seen in hilly areas
red-kite.diet: Carrion, earthworms, small mammals, and birds; an opportunistic and partly scavenging raptor
red-kite.migration: Partially migratory; western birds historically resident, northern mainland European birds winter farther south

common-kestrel.size: Small falcon, roughly pigeon-sized; known for its distinctive hovering flight
common-kestrel.habitat: Open and semi-open country — farmland, roadsides, moorland, and urban areas across Europe, Asia, and Africa
common-kestrel.diet: Small mammals (especially voles), large insects, and occasionally small birds spotted from a hover
common-kestrel.migration: Partially migratory; northern and eastern birds move south in winter; many western European birds are resident

eurasian-hobby.size: Small, slim falcon; roughly the size of a Eurasian kestrel but more streamlined
eurasian-hobby.habitat: Open woodland, heathland, farmland with scattered trees, and wetland edges
eurasian-hobby.diet: Large flying insects (dragonflies, beetles) and small birds including swallows and swifts, caught in aerial pursuit
eurasian-hobby.migration: Strongly migratory; breeds in Eurasia, winters in sub-Saharan Africa

eurasian-eagle-owl.size: Females up to 75 cm long with a wingspan of 188 cm; one of the largest owl species in the world
eurasian-eagle-owl.habitat: Mountainous and rocky areas, forest edges, steppes, and remote areas across Eurasia
eurasian-eagle-owl.diet: Small mammals (rodents, rabbits), birds, and occasionally reptiles, amphibians, and large insects
eurasian-eagle-owl.migration: Largely resident across its wide Eurasian range

tawny-owl.size: Stocky, medium-sized owl; round-headed with brown or grey plumage
tawny-owl.habitat: Woodlands across Europe and western Siberia; also parks, gardens, and urban areas
tawny-owl.diet: Mainly rodents; also small birds, frogs, and invertebrates in urban areas
tawny-owl.migration: Non-migratory and highly territorial; young birds unable to find territories often starve

little-owl.habitat: Farmland, woodland fringes, steppes, and semi-deserts across Europe, Asia, and North Africa
little-owl.diet: Insects, earthworms, other invertebrates, and small vertebrates
little-owl.migration: Largely resident and sedentary across its range

short-eared-owl.size: Medium-sized owl with very short, usually invisible ear tufts
short-eared-owl.habitat: Open grasslands, moorland, tundra, and marshes; a widespread grassland specialist
short-eared-owl.diet: Small mammals, especially voles, caught in low coursing flights over open ground
short-eared-owl.migration: Partially migratory and nomadic; follows prey populations, moving south or to lower ground in winter

long-eared-owl.size: Medium-sized owl with conspicuous long ear tufts when erect
long-eared-owl.habitat: Woodland edges, forest patches near open ground; roosts communally in dense scrub
long-eared-owl.diet: Primarily small rodents, especially voles; can adapt to birds and insects under some conditions
long-eared-owl.migration: Partially migratory and sometimes nomadic; northern populations move south in winter

razorbill.size: Colonial North Atlantic seabird; the only living member of the genus Alca
razorbill.habitat: Open North Atlantic Ocean; nests in crevices and ledges on coastal cliffs
razorbill.diet: Fish and marine invertebrates caught by diving underwater
razorbill.migration: Migratory; disperses to sea in winter away from breeding cliffs

common-guillemot.size: Large auk with a circumpolar distribution; one of the largest members of the auk family
common-guillemot.habitat: Low-Arctic and boreal seas; nests in dense colonies on bare rocky cliff ledges
common-guillemot.diet: Fish (especially sand eels and capelin) and marine invertebrates, caught by deep dives up to 180 m
common-guillemot.migration: Partially migratory; some populations remain near breeding sites year-round, others disperse to sea in winter

black-legged-kittiwake.size: Small, dainty gull named for its distinctive 'kittee-wa-aaake' call
black-legged-kittiwake.habitat: Open ocean and coastal cliffs; breeds in colonies on cliff faces and sea stacks
black-legged-kittiwake.diet: Small fish and marine invertebrates caught near the sea surface
black-legged-kittiwake.migration: Migratory; spends winter at sea away from breeding cliffs, ranging widely over open ocean

european-herring-gull.size: Large gull, up to 66 cm long
european-herring-gull.habitat: Northern and western European coasts, inland areas, and urban environments
european-herring-gull.diet: Fish, crustaceans, some plant matter, carrion, and food scavenged from humans
european-herring-gull.migration: Partially migratory; colder-region birds move south in winter, many coastal birds are resident year-round

great-cormorant.size: Large, dark seabird with a long hooked bill and distinctive upright wing-drying posture
great-cormorant.habitat: Coastal waters, large rivers, lakes, and reservoirs across the Old World and Australasia
great-cormorant.diet: Fish caught by pursuit diving; a highly effective underwater hunter
great-cormorant.migration: Largely resident; some northern birds move to coasts or southward in winter

northern-gannet.size: 87–100 cm long; wingspan 170–180 cm; the largest seabird in the northern Atlantic
northern-gannet.habitat: North Atlantic Ocean; nests in large colonies on offshore islands and sea cliffs
northern-gannet.diet: Primarily fish, caught by high-speed plunge-dives from height into the sea
northern-gannet.migration: Migratory; undertakes seasonal movements, ranging widely across the North Atlantic in winter

common-ostrich.size: The largest living bird; can run at 55 km/h sustained, with bursts up to 70 km/h
common-ostrich.habitat: Savanna, open woodland, and semi-arid plains of Africa
common-ostrich.diet: Mainly plant matter; also invertebrates and small reptiles
common-ostrich.migration: Nomadic rather than migratory; moves in groups of 5–50 birds across the African savanna

secretarybird.size: Very large, eagle-bodied bird on crane-like legs; stands up to 1.3 m tall
secretarybird.habitat: Open grasslands and savanna of sub-Saharan Africa
secretarybird.diet: Insects, small vertebrates, and snakes, killed by powerful stomping on the ground
secretarybird.migration: Largely resident and terrestrial across its sub-Saharan range

lesser-flamingo.size: The smallest flamingo species; smaller and deeper pink than the greater flamingo
lesser-flamingo.habitat: Alkaline and saline lakes and lagoons in sub-Saharan Africa and western India
lesser-flamingo.diet: Blue-green algae and diatoms filtered from alkaline water with its highly specialised bill
lesser-flamingo.migration: Nomadic rather than strictly migratory; moves between suitable saline lakes as conditions change

hamerkop.size: Medium-sized waterbird with brown plumage and a distinctive backward-projecting crest
hamerkop.habitat: Wide variety of wetlands — estuaries, lakesides, fish ponds, riverbanks, and rocky coasts in Africa
hamerkop.diet: Fish and amphibians primarily; also shrimps, insects, and rodents, caught in shallow water
hamerkop.migration: Sedentary; largely resident with only local seasonal movements

sacred-ibis.size: Large wading bird with white plumage and a long, bare black head and neck
sacred-ibis.habitat: Wetlands, river margins, coastal shores, and farmland across much of Africa
sacred-ibis.diet: Fish, frogs, insects, small reptiles, and carrion; probes mud with its curved bill
sacred-ibis.migration: Largely resident across its African range; some seasonal local movements

marabou-stork.size: Very large stork; average wingspan 2.6 m, recorded up to 3.2 m — among the widest of any land bird
marabou-stork.habitat: Sub-Saharan Africa in both wet and arid habitats, often near human habitation and landfill sites
marabou-stork.diet: Carrion, offal, and almost any organic waste; also fish, frogs, and small vertebrates
marabou-stork.migration: Largely resident; some local seasonal movements within sub-Saharan Africa

yellow-billed-stork.size: Large African stork with a distinctive yellow bill and pink-flushed white plumage
yellow-billed-stork.habitat: Wetlands, flooded grasslands, lake margins, and rivers south of the Sahara and in Madagascar
yellow-billed-stork.diet: Fish, frogs, insects, and crustaceans, caught by tactile bill-sweeping in shallow water
yellow-billed-stork.migration: Largely resident; some local movements following water levels and rainfall

grey-crowned-crane.size: Large crane with distinctive stiff golden crown and red throat patch
grey-crowned-crane.habitat: Dry savannah, marshes, cultivated lands, and grassy areas near rivers and lakes in sub-Saharan Africa
grey-crowned-crane.diet: Omnivorous — plants, seeds, grain, insects, frogs, worms, snakes, and small fish
grey-crowned-crane.migration: Largely resident; undertakes some local seasonal movements

martial-eagle.size: One of the largest and most powerful eagles in Africa; hunts primarily from a high soar
martial-eagle.habitat: Wooded belts and savanna across sub-Saharan Africa
martial-eagle.diet: Mammals, birds, and reptiles; an opportunistic predator that stoops on prey from great height
martial-eagle.migration: Largely resident within its sub-Saharan African range

bateleur.size: Medium-sized eagle with very long bow-like wings and a uniquely short tail; large head
bateleur.habitat: Open savanna with scattered trees and open dry woodland in sub-Saharan Africa and Arabia
bateleur.diet: Carrion, small to medium-sized mammals, reptiles, and birds; a free-wheeling generalist predator
bateleur.migration: Largely resident; a highly aerial species that soars widely over its range

african-crowned-eagle.size: Overall length up to 90 cm; considered Africa's most powerful eagle relative to prey size
african-crowned-eagle.habitat: Riparian woodlands and forests of sub-Saharan Africa; also adapts to urban wooded areas
african-crowned-eagle.diet: Primarily mammals — monkeys, antelopes, hyraxes, and rodents; occasionally birds and reptiles
african-crowned-eagle.migration: Largely resident within its sub-Saharan forest and woodland range

white-backed-vulture.size: Large vulture; the most common vulture species in Africa
white-backed-vulture.habitat: Open savanna, grassland, and woodland of sub-Saharan Africa
white-backed-vulture.diet: Carrion; a obligate scavenger that locates carcasses from the air
white-backed-vulture.migration: Largely resident; some dispersal movements across the African savanna

pied-crow.size: Small crow-sized raven; widely distributed African corvid with contrasting black-and-white plumage
pied-crow.habitat: Wide range of open and semi-open habitats across mainland Africa, including urban areas
pied-crow.diet: Omnivorous scavenger — insects, carrion, seeds, fruit, and human food waste
pied-crow.migration: Largely resident across its African range

greater-blue-eared-starling.size: Medium-sized glossy starling with iridescent blue-green plumage
greater-blue-eared-starling.habitat: Open woodland and savanna from Senegal east to Ethiopia and south to South Africa
greater-blue-eared-starling.diet: Fruit, seeds, and insects; a typical open-woodland omnivore
greater-blue-eared-starling.migration: Largely resident with some seasonal movements within its African range

red-billed-oxpecker.size: Small passerine bird; slender with a distinctive bright red bill
red-billed-oxpecker.habitat: Eastern African savanna, closely associated with large mammals like buffalo and rhino
red-billed-oxpecker.diet: Ticks, parasites, and blood from the skin of large African mammals
red-billed-oxpecker.migration: Largely resident in the eastern savanna of sub-Saharan Africa

southern-carmine-bee-eater.size: Large, brilliantly coloured bee-eater with a long central tail spike and crimson plumage
southern-carmine-bee-eater.habitat: Open country, river valleys, and cliffs across sub-equatorial Africa
southern-carmine-bee-eater.diet: Bees and other large flying insects caught in aerial pursuit
southern-carmine-bee-eater.migration: Migratory within Africa; follows seasonal insect availability across sub-equatorial regions

white-fronted-bee-eater.size: Medium-sized bee-eater with a white forehead, red throat, and square tail
white-fronted-bee-eater.habitat: Open country, river banks, and earthen cliffs of sub-equatorial Africa
white-fronted-bee-eater.diet: Bees and other flying insects caught in hawking flights from low perches
white-fronted-bee-eater.migration: Largely resident in sub-equatorial Africa with some local seasonal movements

malachite-kingfisher.size: Tiny, jewel-bright kingfisher; one of the smallest kingfishers in Africa
malachite-kingfisher.habitat: Rivers, lakes, and wetland margins with dense vegetation south of the Sahara
malachite-kingfisher.diet: Small fish and aquatic invertebrates caught by plunge-diving from a low perch
malachite-kingfisher.migration: Largely resident; some climate-related seasonal movements
